name: DATA
description: Multifunction measurement and signal tool. Seven menu-selected programs: oscilloscope, spectrum analyzer, spectrograph, tuner, dual wave output, quad clock output, voltage monitor. Color display with buffered through jacks on all four inputs.

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Push-turn encoder. Twist to scroll menus or parameter values. Push to enter a program, select a parameter, or toggle between list-scroll and value-edit.
controls.detail: Push: select · Twist: scroll/edit · Push+turn in some screens: coarse adjust

controls.name: Menu Button
controls.description: Return to the main menu from any program. Press and hold for ~1.5s inside Oscilloscope to open the secondary top menu (XY Scope, MIN-MODE).
controls.detail: Tap: back to main menu · Hold 1.5s (in Scope): secondary menu

controls.name: Soft Buttons 2-1 – 2-4 (top row)
controls.description: Four context-sensitive buttons along the top of the display. Labels map to the boxes shown at the top edge of the screen for the active program.

controls.name: Soft Buttons 1-1 – 1-4 (bottom row)
controls.description: Four context-sensitive buttons along the bottom of the display. Labels map to the boxes shown at the bottom edge of the screen. 1-4 opens Settings from the main menu.
controls.detail: Bottom row · Left→Right: 1-1, 1-2, 1-3, 1-4 · 1-4 on main menu: Settings

controls.name: Display
controls.description: Full-color LCD. Renders waveforms, FFT graphs, tuner readout, clock parameters, volt meter, and all menus.

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Channel 1 signal input. Clock (Internal Master) mode: Run/Stop gate. Clock (External Sync) mode: external 16th-note clock. Tuner / Scope / Spectrum: measurement input.
inputs.voltage: ±10V · AC or DC coupled per on-screen setting

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Channel 2 signal input. In Clock program (both Internal and External modes) acts as Reset input — send a gate to reset the clock's measure position.
inputs.voltage: ±10V · AC or DC coupled per on-screen setting

inputs.name: IN 3
inputs.description: Channel 3 signal input. In Clock and Wave Output programs doubles as a user-assignable CV input — route to any output's div/mult, offset, frequency, or amplitude with selectable attenuation.
inputs.voltage: ±10V · AC or DC coupled per on-screen setting · CV range ±5V (10Vpp)

inputs.name: IN 4
inputs.description: Channel 4 signal input. In Clock and Wave Output programs doubles as a user-assignable CV input — route to any output parameter with per-destination attenuation.
inputs.voltage: ±10V · AC or DC coupled per on-screen setting · CV range ±5V (10Vpp)

inputs.name: THRU 1–4
inputs.description: Buffered copies of the four inputs. Always live regardless of active program — use DATA as an inline probe without splitting cables.

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Program-dependent. Wave Output channel 1 (audio/LFO), Clock Output 1 (trigger), Voltage Monitor channel 1 CV or gate. Silent in Scope/Tuner/Spectrum programs.
outputs.voltage: Wave: ±5V (10Vpp) · Clock: 0V to +5V · Volt Monitor CV: −5V to +10V

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Program-dependent. Wave Output channel 2 (audio/LFO), Clock Output 2, Voltage Monitor channel 2.
outputs.voltage: Wave: ±5V · Clock: 0V to +5V · Volt Monitor CV: −5V to +10V

outputs.name: OUT 3
outputs.description: Program-dependent. Clock Output 3 or Voltage Monitor channel 3 (CV / momentary gate / latched gate). Silent in Scope, Tuner, Spectrum, and Wave Output programs.
outputs.voltage: Clock: 0V to +5V · Volt Monitor CV: −5V to +10V · Gate: user-set HI/LOW

outputs.name: OUT 4
outputs.description: Program-dependent. Clock Output 4 or Voltage Monitor channel 4 (CV / momentary gate / latched gate). Silent in Scope, Tuner, Spectrum, and Wave Output programs.
outputs.voltage: Clock: 0V to +5V · Volt Monitor CV: −5V to +10V · Gate: user-set HI/LOW

behaviors.name: Program switching
behaviors.trigger: Menu button, then encoder, push to enter
behaviors.description: Only one program runs at a time. Switching programs re-assigns the meaning of IN 1–4 and what OUT 1–4 emit. The buffered THRU jacks keep passing signal regardless of program.

behaviors.name: Oscilloscope — four channels + triggering
behaviors.description: Four channel traces at once. TIME scale 50µs to 5.0s per division (12 divisions full screen, so 600µs to 60s total window). Per-channel SCALE 1V/2V/5V per division, AC/DC coupling, vertical position. Trigger modes AUTO (free-run) and NORM (triggers on crossing LEVEL with RISE or FALL edge); AUTO recommended for slow signals.

behaviors.name: Oscilloscope cursors
behaviors.description: Two cursors per axis — AX/BX on time, AY/BY on voltage — with live delta (BX-AX, BY-AY) readout. Y cursor references any of the four channel SCALEs on the fly. DISPLAY parameter keeps cursors visible while the pop-up is closed.

behaviors.name: Oscilloscope XY Scope
behaviors.trigger: Scope · hold Menu ~1.5s, then XY SCOPE
behaviors.description: Renders a Lissajous pair: CH1(X) & CH2(Y), or CH3(X) & CH4(Y). Independent scale/offset per channel via the XY SCOPE pop-up so the XY figure and the waveform traces can be sized separately.

behaviors.name: Oscilloscope Minimal Display Mode
behaviors.description: Hides grid, channel labels, and soft-button legends — just the traces. All soft buttons disabled except the small MIN-MODE indicator in the top-left; click it again to exit.

behaviors.name: Tuner
behaviors.trigger: Tuner program · signal on selected input channel
behaviors.description: Displays nearest chromatic note, target frequency, measured frequency, and Hz difference. Any of IN 1–4 can be picked via bottom soft buttons. Typical accuracy ±0.01 Hz. Range 27.50 Hz to 2,960 Hz (A0 to ~F#7). Push 2-1 NORMALIZE for small signals (≤6Vpp).

behaviors.name: Voltage Monitor — 4 CV/Gate sources
behaviors.trigger: Voltage Monitor program · OUTPUT pop-up (2-4), then channel button 1-1 to 1-4
behaviors.description: Each of OUT 1–4 is independently set to CV (constant −5V to +10V), GATE-MO (gate while button held), or GATE-LA (latching toggle). Gate HI / LOW voltages are user-configurable per channel. IN 1–4 are scoped live on the display. Outputs retain their set values when switching to Tuner / Scope / Spectral programs.

behaviors.name: Wave Output — dual generator
behaviors.description: Two precision generators on OUT 1 and OUT 2. Shapes: SINE, SQR, SAW, TRI. Per-channel FREQ (0.01 Hz to 9,999.99 Hz manual; 0.01 Hz to 4,200 Hz under CV), PHASE, AMP (−200% to +200%, negative inverts), OFFSET (−100% to +100%, re-centers the wave). Output ±5V (10Vpp).

behaviors.name: Wave Output — 1V/oct pitch tracking
behaviors.trigger: Wave Output · CV pop-up (2-3) assigns an input to WAVE1/WAVE2 FREQ, MODE set to 1V/OCT
behaviors.description: Set the base TUNE to note C0 (16.35 Hz), then CV IN accepts 1V/oct over C0 to C8 (8 octaves). Normal functional CV range 0V to +8V; larger inputs up to +10V still produce sound but become unstable above ~15,800 Hz.

behaviors.name: Wave Output — waveshaping (CLIP / FOLD)
behaviors.trigger: Wave Output · MODE pop-up (2-4) · SHAPE · CLIP or FOLD
behaviors.description: CLIP squashes the wave flat once AMP × OFFSET pushes past ±5V — useful on sines and saws (no effect on square). FOLD reflects the waveform back when it passes ±5V; in FOLD mode the AMP control is relabeled FOLD and 50% produces an unmodified wave, 100% a first reflection.

behaviors.name: Clock Output — 4 outputs, 32 div/mult values
behaviors.description: Four clock outputs with per-channel DIV/MULT (32 values from /48 to ×48, including triplets like ×3, ×6 4T, ×12 8T, ×48 128T) and OFFSET (±96 ticks, one quarter note). Internal Master (set BPM) or External Sync (IN 1 expects 16th-note pulses / PPQN 4). Press 1-4 for RUN/STOP, 1-3 for RESET; IN 2 is Reset gate in both modes. Clock outs 0V to +5V.

behaviors.name: Clock Output — CV-assignable IN 3 / IN 4
behaviors.description: IN 3 or IN 4 (range ±5V / 10Vpp) can modulate any Clock 1–4 DIV/MULT or OFFSET, with independent attenuation per destination. One CV source can drive several outputs at once for complex polyrhythmic modulation.

behaviors.name: Spectrum Analyzer & Spectrograph
behaviors.description: FFT of the selected input channel (switch with bottom soft buttons). Spectrum draws bar-graph magnitude vs frequency with the peak bin's frequency read out at top. Spectrograph scrolls the same FFT as a color-coded heat map over time. Window type (square, Hann, etc.) selectable via 2-1.

behaviors.name: Memory — 8 system-state snapshots
behaviors.trigger: Settings (1-4 on main menu) · MEMORY page
behaviors.description: Eight slots save a snapshot of every program's current state (Scope channel positions and TIME, Wave Output shape/freq/amp, Clock BPM, etc). 1-1 SAVE, 1-4 LOAD. Useful for recall between test routines or live performances.

behaviors.name: Front-panel reset
behaviors.description: Reboots DATA without cycling rack power. Same effect as a power cycle, but only affects DATA. Useful if a program locks or firmware misbehaves.

behaviors.name: microSD firmware update
behaviors.trigger: Place .bin (DSxxxxxx for system or DBxxxxxx for bootloader) on microSD root, reinsert, power up
behaviors.description: System firmware auto-detects on boot. Bootloader firmware updates via Settings → MISC → BOOTLOADER FIRMWARE UPDATE. Current system v01.02.05 (230107) fixes NORM trigger lock-up on low-freq signals at fast timebase.

behaviors.name: Calibration routines
behaviors.description: AUTO CALIBRATION recalibrates the ADCs using internally-routed reference voltages — run after ~30 min warm-up when moving to a new environment. MANUAL CALIBRATION of the DACs requires a sub-millivolt DMM on OUT 1–4 and is rarely needed after factory burn-in.

patchIdeas.name: Calibrate an analog VCO
patchIdeas.patch: Use the Tuner program to trim an analog VCO's 1V/oct scaling against DATA's reference.
steps: Press Menu, scroll with the encoder to Tuner, push to enter.
Patch your VCO's main out into DATA IN 1 (channel 1 should be highlighted in the bottom bar).
Patch a steady 0V (or a known C2 reference) into the VCO's V/oct, read the note on DATA, trim the VCO's offset/tune until it reads C2 exactly.
Patch a +3V reference (from a precision source) into the VCO V/oct — DATA should now read C5.
If it reads flat or sharp, adjust the VCO's scale trimmer until C5 lines up with 0.00 Hz NOTE DIF.
Repeat the 0V / +3V check until both octaves lock within a few cents (Hz equivalent).
If the VCO output is weak (<6Vpp), press 2-1 to engage NORMALIZE for cleaner detection.
patchIdeas.signalOut: None (measurement patch). DATA IN 1 reads note name, measured frequency, and Hz difference.
patchIdeas.hear: Tuner display shows nearest note name and Hz difference. A well-calibrated VCO holds within a few Hz across three octaves. Drift beyond a few Hz at C5 signals scale trim is off.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Tuner
* Input: IN 1

VCO:

Reference:

- Reference (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> DATA (IN 1)

patchIdeas.name: Quad master clock
patchIdeas.patch: Drive four destinations from DATA's Clock Output at related divisions — whole notes, quarters, eighths, and triplets.
steps: Press Menu, scroll to Clock Output, push to enter.
Set BPM with the encoder (e.g., 120) in Internal Master mode.
Assign OUT 1 to ×1 (quarter notes), OUT 2 to ×2 (8th notes), OUT 3 to ×3 (8th-note triplets, labelled 8T), OUT 4 to /4 (whole notes).
Patch OUT 1 into your main drum trigger.
Patch OUT 2 into a hi-hat trigger.
Patch OUT 3 into a percussion voice for a triplet layer.
Patch OUT 4 into an envelope that opens a pad once per bar.
Press soft-button 1-4 (RUN/STOP) to start; 1-3 (RESET) snaps all four outputs to beat one.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked polyrhythm: four-on-the-floor kick, doubled hats, a triplet percussion pattern, and a slow pad swell every bar. All phases line up on downbeat after pressing RESET.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Clock Output
* Mode: Internal Master
* BPM: 120
* OUT 1: x1
* OUT 2: x2
* OUT 3: x3 (8T)
* OUT 4: /4

Drum:

Hats:

Perc:

Envelope:

- DATA (OUT 1) c> Drum (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 2) c> Hats (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 3) c> Perc (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 4) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Dual LFO pair with phase offset
patchIdeas.patch: Use Wave Output for two slow LFOs with a deliberate phase offset — stereo-style movement from one module.
steps: Press Menu, select Wave Output.
WAVE 1: TYPE sine, FREQ 0.20 Hz, AMP 100%, PHASE 0, OFFSET 0%.
WAVE 2: TYPE sine, FREQ 0.20 Hz, AMP 100%, PHASE 90, OFFSET 0%.
Patch OUT 1 to the filter cutoff CV of your left-channel VCF.
Patch OUT 2 to the filter cutoff CV of your right-channel VCF.
Run audio through both filters into a stereo mixer.
Adjust WAVE 2 PHASE between 0 and 180 to taste — 90 gives the widest stereo feel, 180 inverts for full anti-phase.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 and OUT 2 — bipolar CV, ±5V sines at 0.2 Hz.
patchIdeas.hear: The two filters sweep in a lazy rotation. At 90° phase the stereo image pans as the cutoff opens and closes on opposite sides. Pull phase to 180° for full inversion, to 0° for mono sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Wave Output
* WAVE 1: sine, 0.2Hz, 100%, 0deg
* WAVE 2: sine, 0.2Hz, 100%, 90deg

VCF L:

VCF R:

- DATA (OUT 1) >> VCF L (Cutoff)
- DATA (OUT 2) >> VCF R (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: A4 reference tone for drift testing
patchIdeas.patch: Park DATA on 440 Hz and A/B-check an analog VCO for warm-up drift over 20 minutes.
steps: Menu → Wave Output. WAVE 1: TYPE sine, FREQ 440.00 Hz, AMP 100%, OFFSET 0%.
Patch DATA OUT 1 into one channel of a mixer.
Patch your analog VCO into the other channel; tune by ear until the beating stops.
Patch the mixer output — or the VCO directly — into DATA IN 1, switch to Tuner to read the offset in Hz.
Record the VCO's starting note and NOTE DIF reading.
Leave the rack running; check back every 5 minutes for 20 minutes.
Watch how far the analog VCO drifts against DATA's digital 440 Hz — this maps the warm-up behaviour of your oscillator.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DATA OUT 1 — sine reference at 440.00 Hz, ±5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A steady pure tone from DATA. The unpatched analog VCO slowly drifts as it warms up — beating against DATA's tone builds, then disappears as you retune. Tuner reads the exact Hz gap between the two.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Wave Output then Tuner
* WAVE 1: sine, 440Hz, 100%

VCO:

Mixer:

- DATA (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In 1)
- VCO (Out) -> Mixer (In 2)
- VCO (Out) -> DATA (IN 1)

patchIdeas.name: Bench voltage source
patchIdeas.patch: Use Voltage Monitor as a four-channel DC source for poking at a new module's CV response.
steps: Menu → Voltage Monitor.
Push soft-button 2-4 (OUTPUT) to open the channel output pop-up.
Set OUT 1 TYPE to CV, dial VOLTAGE to +1.00V with the encoder (push-turn for coarse).
Repeat for OUT 2, OUT 3, OUT 4 at +2.00V, +3.00V, and −2.00V.
Leave the OUTPUT pop-up — the four live values show at the bottom of the screen.
Patch OUT 1 into the CV input of the module under test; swap between OUT 1–4 to confirm each voltage response.
Patch the module's output back into DATA IN 1 — the Voltage Monitor displays it scoped live on the top panel.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — constant DC, user-set between −5V and +10V.
patchIdeas.hear: No audio — this is a measurement patch. The module under test should respond predictably at +1V, +2V, +3V (equal increments if the CV path is linear). IN 1–4 scope each reading live on the display.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Voltage Monitor
* OUT 1: CV +1V
* OUT 2: CV +2V
* OUT 3: CV +3V
* OUT 4: CV -2V

DUT:

- DATA (OUT 1) >> DUT (CV In)
- DUT (Out) -> DATA (IN 1)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral filter-sweep visualiser
patchIdeas.patch: Watch a VCF's resonance peak traverse the spectrum as an LFO sweeps its cutoff.
steps: Menu → Spectrograph.
Patch a noise source into your VCF input.
Patch an LFO (0.1 Hz triangle) into the VCF cutoff CV.
Turn VCF resonance high but below self-oscillation.
Patch the VCF output into DATA IN 1 (use the bottom soft buttons to confirm channel 1 is the FFT source).
Let it run — the spectrograph paints the cutoff peak travelling up and down the frequency axis over time.
Swap to Spectrum Analyzer program to see a live bar-graph snapshot with peak-bin frequency read out instead of the scrolling heat map.
patchIdeas.signalOut: None (measurement patch). DATA displays the spectrum of IN 1.
patchIdeas.hear: A slowly sweeping whooshing noise through the filter. On the display, a bright band climbs and falls on the frequency axis tracing the resonant peak; low-level noise stays dim, the peak stays bright.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Spectrograph

Noise:

VCF:
* Resonance: high

LFO:
* Rate: 0.1Hz triangle

- Noise (Out) -> VCF (In)
- LFO (Out) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- VCF (Out) -> DATA (IN 1)

patchIdeas.name: External-synced poly-clock
patchIdeas.patch: Chain DATA behind a master sequencer clock so every DATA output divides or multiplies that clock, with IN 2 as reset.
steps: Menu → Clock Output. Open MODE (2-4) and set External Sync.
Patch your master clock (DAW / sequencer / Pam's) into DATA IN 1 — DATA expects PPQN 4 (16th-note pulses).
Patch the master sequencer's reset into DATA IN 2 so downbeats realign.
Assign OUT 1 ×1 (quarters), OUT 2 /2 (half notes), OUT 3 ×4 (16ths), OUT 4 ×3 (8T triplets).
Patch OUT 1 into a bass drum trigger.
Patch OUT 2 into a half-time snare.
Patch OUT 3 into a fast ratchet voice; OUT 4 into a triplet perc.
Start/stop the master — DATA follows. Triggering the master's reset snaps all four outputs back to beat one.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — trigger pulses, 0V to +5V, locked to the external clock.
patchIdeas.hear: The whole rhythm locks to your master clock. Stopping the master stops all four DATA outputs. Triggering reset snaps everything onto the downbeat together — useful after tempo changes or live edits.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Clock Output
* Mode: External Sync (PPQN 4)
* OUT 1: x1
* OUT 2: /2
* OUT 3: x4
* OUT 4: x3 (8T)

Master Clock:

Reset Source:

Kick:

Snare:

Ratchet:

Perc:

- Master Clock (Out) c> DATA (IN 1)
- Reset Source (Out) t> DATA (IN 2)
- DATA (OUT 1) c> Kick (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 2) c> Snare (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 3) c> Ratchet (Trig)
- DATA (OUT 4) c> Perc (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Debug an unknown cable
patchIdeas.patch: Park DATA in Scope to identify what's on a mystery cable — CV, gate, audio, or nothing.
steps: Menu → Oscilloscope.
Patch the mystery cable into DATA IN 1.
Push channel 1's bottom button to open its pop-up; set DC coupling, SCALE 5V/div as a starting point.
Push 2-4 (TIME) and turn the encoder to set 10MS/div (then adjust).
Set TRIG mode AUTO (top-row 2-2) until you know what's on the line — NORM hides non-triggering signals.
Read the trace: flat line = DC voltage, slow wave = LFO, fast wave = audio, pulse = gate/trigger.
Zoom the time scale — 5.0S/div for slow LFOs, 1MS/div for audio.
Toggle AC coupling if a large DC offset is hiding a small AC component.
Push 2-1 (CUR) and enable cursors to measure exact period or amplitude.
patchIdeas.signalOut: None (measurement patch). DATA displays the trace of IN 1.
patchIdeas.hear: No audio — visual identification. Flat trace at +2V means DC CV; a regular 1 Hz triangle means an LFO; a 4 kHz sine means audio. A single brief step means a trigger. Read amplitude from the Y cursor, period from the X cursor.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DATA:
* Program: Oscilloscope
* CH 1: DC, 5V/div
* TIME: 10ms/div
* TRIG: AUTO

Mystery:

- Mystery (Out) >> DATA (IN 1)
